  "findings": [
    "Sling angle is 25 degrees, which is below the industry standard minimum of 30 degrees.",
    "Calculated tension per leg is 2,366.2 lbs, which is within the 3,500 lbs WLL, but the low angle creates severe stability risks and violates safety standards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current rigging plan.",
    "Increase the sling angle to at least 45 or 60 degrees by using longer slings or a spreader bar."
  ],
  "rationale": "The tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle) = (2000 / 2) / sin(25) = 1000 / 0.4226 = 2,366.2 lbs. While this tension is below the 3,500 lbs vertical WLL of each leg, ASME B30.9 and OSHA standards strongly prohibit or advise against using horizontal sling angles of less than 30 degrees due to extreme tension multiplication and load instability.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
